data.title: Columbia Banking System to release Q3 2025 results on Oct 30 after market close. Conference call to follow.
data.description: Columbia Banking System, Inc., the parent company of Columbia Bank, is set to disclose its financial results for the third quarter of 2025 on Thursday, October 30, 2025, post-market hours. The official announcement comes from Tacoma, Washington, and the company is listed as "Columbia" on the Nasdaq exchange under the ticker symbol COLB.

data.description: The economic approval ratings of President Donald Trump are beginning to mirror those of the previous president.

data.title: ChangAn hosts Global Service Skills Competition in Bangkok with 45 contestants from 19 countries to showcase expertise.
data.description: ChangAn Automobile held its inaugural Global Service Skills Competition in Bangkok, Thailand on August 27-28, 2025. The event featured 45 contestants from 19 countries showcasing their skills in service, engine mechanical troubleshooting, and vehicle electrical system troubleshooting. This competition highlighted ChangAn's professional service capabilities and talent development internationally.

data.title: Las Vegas housing supply up 31%, prices dip slightly due to 8.6% drop in pending sales, triple national increase.
data.description: In Seattle, the housing market witnessed a significant development as the total number of homes for sale in Las Vegas surged by 31% compared to the previous year in July. This increase is the largest among major U.S. metro areas and approximately three times higher than the national average. The findings are from a recent report by Redfin, a real estate brokerage supported by Rocket. Notably, this marks the second consecutive year of experiencing inventory spikes exceeding 20% in Las Vegas. The escalating supply in the region is attributed to potential homebuyers hesitating to make purchases, leading to a buildup in available properties. Concurrently, pending home sales in Las Vegas saw a decline of 8.6% year over year.

data.title: Celsius Holdings and PepsiCo sign agreement to strengthen long-term partnership. #CELH #LiveFit
data.description: A new agreement has been made between Celsius Holdings and PepsiCo to enhance their ongoing strategic partnership. The deal, announced in Boca Raton, Florida, and Purchase, New York, aims to solidify their collaboration for the future. The precise details of the agreement and the specific enhancements it will bring were not disclosed to the public. This move signifies a commitment to continuing and strengthening the relationship between the two companies. The partnership between Celsius Holdings and PepsiCo is integral to both companies’ strategies moving forward, emphasizing a shared vision for growth and success in the market.

data.title: Fed meeting today likely to reveal more hawkish interest-rate outlook. New concern to be addressed.
data.description: The upcoming Federal Reserve meeting is expected to show a more aggressive stance on short-term interest rates. There is speculation of a hawkish shift in policy direction as concerns mount. This adjustment could have significant implications for financial markets and investors.

data.title: Burlington Stores, Inc. exceeds Q2 fiscal 2025 expectations; revenue at $2.705 billion, up 10%; comp store sales up 5%. Analysts raise forecasts.
data.description: Burlington Stores, Inc. (NYSE: BURL) exceeded expectations in their second-quarter fiscal 2025 report. Quarterly revenue reached $2.705 billion, marking a 10% increase from the previous year and surpassing the estimated $2.652 billion. Comparable store sales saw a 5% increase. Net income stood at $94 million, translating to $1.47 per diluted share, compared to $74 million and $1.15 per share respectively from the prior year. Adjusted earnings were $1.72 per share, a significant improvement from the $1.24 reported last year and beating the anticipated $1.32. CEO Michael O'Sullivan noted a 5% rise in comparable store sales, building on the growth from last year. The company also witnessed strong margin and earnings performance, with adjusted EBIT Margin rising by 120 basis points.

data.title: Emergent BioSolutions boosts Naloxone awareness via campaign on International Overdose Awareness Day, focusing on college students.
data.description: On August 31, 2025, Gaithersburg, Maryland - In observance of International Overdose Awareness Day, Emergent BioSolutions (NYSE: EBS) is reintroducing its Ready to Rescue public awareness initiative. This campaign focuses on educating young adults on opioid emergencies and stresses the significance of having NARCAN® Nasal Spray on hand. The program will target college and university students, particularly those involved in Greek Life, wellness services, and athletic programs. Pro Football Hall of Famer Emmitt Smith and Zac Clark, the founder and CEO of Release Recovery, will spearhead discussions aimed at dispelling the stigma surrounding accidental opioid poisonings. Their goal is to encourage students to equip themselves with over-the-counter NARCAN® Nasal Spray and be prepared to potentially save a life.

data.title: QYOU Media Inc. reported positive Adjusted EBITDA for Q2 FY 2025, with 80% net loss improvement. Momentum towards public listing on BSE MSE platform.
data.description: A recent report showcased an impressive 80% decrease in net losses, highlighting the advantages of a strategic shift towards businesses catering to the creator economy. The positive momentum of Chatterbox Technologies is propelling them closer to their goal of going public on the BSE MSE platform. This news comes from a joint announcement made in Toronto, Los Angeles, and Mumbai on August 29, 2025, by QYOU Media Inc.

data.title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Market Report predicts revenue exceeding $480.1 million in 2025, with significant growth expected until 2035 due to quantum threat.
data.description: A report on the Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) Market covering the years 2025-2035 has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The global revenue for the PQC Market is anticipated to exceed US$480.1 million by 2025, with a steady increase expected up to 2035. The primary catalyst for the growth of the PQC market is the imminent risk posed by quantum computers to traditional encryption techniques like RSA and ECC (Elliptic Curve Cryptography).
